paper_title: Bayesian Uncertainty Quantification with Anchored Ensembles for Robust EV Power Consumption Prediction
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of driving-range uncertainty in Battery Electric Vehicles (BEVs) by developing a robust model for energy consumption prediction. Using real-world telematics data, the authors employ a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network to estimate instantaneous power consumption. The core contribution is a comprehensive Uncertainty Quantification (UQ) analysis that captures both aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty. They adopt a Bayesian ensembling method extended to LSTMs to model epistemic uncertainty and incorporate a probabilistic output layer to model aleatoric uncertainty, resulting in predictive intervals suitable for online range management and decision-making.
research_questions_or_objectives: To estimate instantaneous power consumption using an LSTM network.
To perform a comprehensive Uncertainty Quantification (UQ) analysis for EV power prediction.
To capture and model both aleatoric and epistemic sources of uncertainty.
To generate predictive intervals suitable for online range management in EVs.

uncertainty_quantification.approach: Bayesian ensembling with a probabilistic output layer
uncertainty_quantification.epistemic_uncertainty_method: A Bayesian ensembling method [pearce2020uncertainty] is adopted and extended to LSTM architectures to capture uncertainty from limited or biased training data.
uncertainty_quantification.aleatoric_uncertainty_method: A probabilistic output layer is incorporated to model uncertainty arising from inherent variability in driving conditions and sensor noise.
uncertainty_quantification.goal: To produce predictive intervals that track error scales for reliable online range management.

notes: The paper focuses exclusively on LSTM models, having previously benchmarked them against other architectures (TCNs, Transformers, Random Forest) in prior work [yahyaabadi2025deep].
title: Bayesian Uncertainty Quantification with Anchored Ensembles for Robust EV Power Consumption Prediction
